name: Ashton Dulin
headline: Deep-shot special-teamer with no real receiving role
body:
Dulin's 2025 line tells the whole story: 12 games, a 21.3% snap share, and just 73 routes run for a 12.7% route rate. He drew only 10 targets (1.9% target share), caught 5 for 106 yards and no scores, and his 0.054 WOPR confirms he sits at the very bottom of Indianapolis' pecking order. The one bright spot is efficiency on his limited usage — a 15.8 air-yards-per-target mark and 21.2 yards per reception, with three grabs of 20-plus and a 48-yard long, plus a tidy 0.576 EPA per play. He's a vertical-only chess piece, not a volume threat.

That profile leaves nothing for fantasy. He spent time on IR with a hamstring issue and projects as a back-end depth receiver and special-teamer rather than someone with a path to targets. Modest projections of roughly 22 catches and 379 yards reflect garbage-time upside at best. Leave him on the wire.
